seoTitle: Strathalbyn vs Stratham — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Strathalbyn and Stratham. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Strathalbyn and Stratham.
heading: Strathalbyn vs Stratham — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Strathalbyn, Western Australia and Stratham, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stratham has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Strathalbyn (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Strathalbyn or Stratham safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Strathalbyn has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Stratham scores 33/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Strathalbyn scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Stratham scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Strathalbyn or Stratham?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stratham has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.83% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
